summary: PR 2475 split the tail range 0x80084E1C-0x8008521C out of `ft_081B` into a new fighter unit, `ft_084E`, and linked both objects as matching. The change moved 14 existing fighter movement/velocity helper functions into `src/melee/ft/ft_084E.c`, added `ft_084E.h`, adjusted GALE01 text and `.sdata2` split ranges/symbols, and changed `configure.py` so `ft_081B.c` and `ft_084E.c` are both `Object(Matching, ...)`; the decomp.dev bot reported +14052 linked-code bytes and 16 new matches.

key_files.role: New C unit containing the moved functions from `ft_80084E1C` through `ft_80085204`.
New header with address-commented prototypes for the new `ft_084E` unit.
Removed the tail block of 14 functions now owned by `ft_084E.c`.
Removed the moved prototypes and temporarily included `ft_084E.h` with a TODO noting a transitive include hack.
Changed `ft_081B.c` to end at text 0x80084E1C and `.sdata2` 0x804D83B8, then added `ft_084E.c` from text 0x80084E1C-0x8008521C and `.sdata2` 0x804D83B8-0x804D83C0.
Retagged local `.sdata2` labels at 0x804D83A8-0x804D83B8 from `ft_804D83A8`-style names to `@195`, `@333`, `@1111`, `@1153`, and `@204`.
Changed `melee/ft/ft_081B.c` from `NonMatching` to `Matching` and added `melee/ft/ft_084E.c` as `Matching`.

what_changed: Added `src/melee/ft/ft_084E.h` with prototypes for the new split unit and kept the address-comment convention such as `/* 084E1C */`.
Removed those moved prototypes from `ft_081B.h`, while adding `#include <melee/ft/ft_084E.h>` with the explicit comment `TODO transitive include hack, should be included directly by dependers`.

Split the GALE01 ranges so `melee/ft/ft_081B.c` covers text 0x80081B38-0x80084E1C and `.sdata2` 0x804D83A8-0x804D83B8, while `melee/ft/ft_084E.c` covers text 0x80084E1C-0x8008521C and `.sdata2` 0x804D83B8-0x804D83C0.
Updated `configure.py` so `ft_081B.c` is now linked as `Matching` instead of `NonMatching`, and the new `ft_084E.c` is also linked as `Matching`.
Updated `.sdata2` symbol records near 0x804D83A8-0x804D83B8 from file-address placeholder names to local `@NNN` labels.
smart_moves: The PR used an object-boundary split rather than rewriting the functions: the moved bodies stayed essentially the same while the split/link configuration made the units match.
It updated all three required link surfaces together: `splits.txt` ranges, `symbols.txt` local data labels, and `configure.py` object matching status.
The split boundary was placed exactly at `ft_80084E1C`, producing a clean `ft_084E` unit and allowing the remaining `ft_081B` unit to become `Matching`.
The temporary transitive include in `ft_081B.h` avoided breaking dependers immediately, but the TODO clearly records that future cleanup should include `ft_084E.h` directly.
The `.sdata2` range was split with the code range, which preserved local constant ownership for the new object.
decomp_lessons: When carving out a new decompilation unit, update text ranges, `.sdata2` ranges, local symbol labels, headers, and `configure.py` in one PR; missing any one of those can leave the object nonmatching or unlinked.
A decomp.dev report showing old-unit broken matches can be expected after moving functions to a new unit; here the same functions reappeared as 100% matches under `main/melee/ft/ft_084E`.
For split files named by address range, keep function names and prototype address comments unchanged so call sites and symbol search remain stable.
Transitive include hacks can be used as a staged migration, but they should be explicitly marked so later PRs can replace indirect header dependencies.
Local `.sdata2` symbols may need to be renamed from address-style placeholders to compiler-local `@NNN` symbols when a unit boundary changes.
naming_conventions: New split file follows the address-prefix convention: `ft_084E.c` and `ft_084E.h` start at the 0x80084E1C region.
Functions retained address-based names such as `ft_80084E1C`, `ft_80085030`, and `ft_80085204`.
Header guard for the new header is `MELEE_FT_084E_H`.
Prototypes use address comments like `/* 085030 */ void ft_80085030(...)`.
Local `.sdata2` symbols in `symbols.txt` use `@195`, `@333`, `@1111`, `@1153`, and `@204` rather than `ft_804D83A8`-style names after the split.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched by splitting/linking the correct object range instead of changing the function logic.
Preserved matching-sensitive source details in the moved code, including `PAD_STACK(8)` in `ft_80084F3C`.
Kept the same float style and control flow in moved functions, including `0.0F`, `ABS(...)`, `cosf`, and `sinf` usage.
Aligned `.sdata2` ownership with the new text unit boundary at 0x80084E1C/0x804D83B8.
Changed `Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/ft_081B.c")` to `Object(Matching, ...)` and added `Object(Matching, "melee/ft/ft_084E.c")` only after the split ranges were declared.

review_feedback.feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in this PR slice.
Reported GALE01 linked code at 38.75% with +0.36% and +14052 bytes, matched data +24 bytes, and linked data +24 bytes.
Listed 16 new matches, including 14 functions and `.sdata2` under `main/melee/ft/ft_084E`, plus `main/melee/ft/ft_081B` `.sdata2` reaching 100%.
Listed 14 broken matches under the old `main/melee/ft/ft_081B` ownership for the same moved functions; based on the diff, these are relocation artifacts rather than evidence of lost matching.

follow_up_queries: Find files still relying on `ft_081B.h` to obtain `ft_084E.h` declarations and include `ft_084E.h` directly.
Search for later PRs that remove the `TODO transitive include hack` from `src/melee/ft/ft_081B.h`.
Look for similar fighter split PRs where a nonmatching object becomes matching by moving a tail address range into `ft_XXXX.c`.
Check whether the local `.sdata2` labels `@195`, `@333`, `@1111`, `@1153`, and `@204` later receive semantic names.
Compare future splits around neighboring files `ft_0819.c`, `ft_084E.c`, and `ft_0852.c` for reusable range/symbol update patterns.
